query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "geneb: why genomic models are hard to compare" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (d) as a foundational methodology reference for the "comparative analysis of genomic models" aspect of the user's question, and criterion (b) by providing an alternative computational framework (alignment-free, frequency-domain) for measuring similarity in DNA sequences, which is the core domain of the user's inquiry into why genomic models are hard to compare.
